**Fidelis Andria 2018** or simply **Fidelis Andria** is an Italian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Andria](/wiki/Andria "Andria"), [Apulia](/wiki/Apulia "Apulia"). The club was founded in 1971 and re-founded in 2005, 2013 and 2018. The team competes in [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") Group H.

Fidelis Andria play their home matches at [Stadio Degli Ulivi](/wiki/Stadio_Degli_Ulivi "Stadio Degli Ulivi"), which has a capacity of 9,140. The stadium is located in [Andria](/wiki/Andria "Andria") itself.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### A.S. Fidelis Andria (1971–2005)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: A.S. Fidelis Andria (1971–2005)")]

The club was founded in 1971 as **A.S. Fidelis Andria** (*Associazione Sportiva Fidelis Andria*). At that time, the club wore red and yellow, and it was the second club in town.

In 1978, following the collapse of A.S. Andria, the club decided to adopt blue and white, the traditional colours of the town.

With these colours, the club played for years in the [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") and [Serie C1](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione"): last playing in Serie B in 1999.

After the 2004–05 season, the club declared bankruptcy.

### A.S. Andria BAT (2005–2013)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: A.S. Andria BAT (2005–2013)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/2/2c/AS_Andria_BAT_logo.png/120px-AS_Andria_BAT_logo.png)](/wiki/File:AS_Andria_BAT_logo.png)

AS Andria BAT crest

In summer 2005, the club was refounded as **A.S. Andria BAT**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (*Associazione Sportiva Andria BAT*). The BAT stood for Barletta-Andria-Trani, as in the [province of Barletta-Andria-Trani](/wiki/Province_of_Barletta-Andria-Trani "Province of Barletta-Andria-Trani").

The team played in the [Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") from the 2005–06 to 2008–09 seasons.

At the end of the [2008–09 Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "2008–09 Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") season, the team was admitted into [Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione") after the forced relegation of [Avellino](/wiki/U.S._Avellino_1912 "U.S. Avellino 1912") in [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D"). At the end of the [2012–13 Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "2012–13 Lega Pro Prima Divisione") season, the club was relegated after the play-offs, but didn't enroll in the [Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") league.

### S.S. Fidelis Andria 1928 (2013–2018)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: S.S. Fidelis Andria 1928 (2013–2018)")]

In its place, in summer 2013, a new team called **S.S.D. Fidelis Andria 1928** was founded and admitted to [Eccellenza Apulia](/wiki/Eccellenza "Eccellenza").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The team ended the season 2013–2014 in 2nd place. After winning the national play-offs, Fidelis Andria were promoted to [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D"), the top level of the Italian non-professional football association.

In season 2014–15, Fidelis Andria played in Group H of [Serie D](/wiki/2014-15_Serie_D "2014-15 Serie D"). On April 26, after winning 3–2 away at [Cavese](/wiki/Cavese "Cavese"), the club was promoted to [Lega Pro](/wiki/Lega_Pro "Lega Pro"), the third-highest football division in Italy, two matches before the end of the regular season. After the regular season, Fidelis Andria took part in the [Scudetto](/wiki/Scudetto "Scudetto") Serie D tournament, in order to assign the amateur champions' title. The team was eliminated in the group stages.

Since July 1, 2015, having become a member of the Italian Professional Football League (*Lega Italiana Calcio Professionistico*), commonly known as [Lega Pro](/wiki/Lega_Pro "Lega Pro"), the club changed its name in **S.S. Fidelis Andria 1928**.

Fidelis Andria ended the season [2015–16](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Lega_Pro "2015–16 Lega Pro") at 7th place, which allowed the club to participate in the next [2016–17 Coppa Italia](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Coppa_Italia "2016–17 Coppa Italia"). On May 7, after the last match of the season in [Catania](/wiki/Catania_Calcio "Catania Calcio"), boss [Luca D'Angelo](/wiki/Luca_D%27Angelo "Luca D'Angelo") announced he wouldn't renew his contract with the club.

### Fidelis Andria 2018 (2018–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Fidelis Andria 2018 (2018–present)")]

In July 2018 the club was declared bankrupt and was denied registration for the upcoming Serie C, based on Covisoc's assessment.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) On the date of the deadline for Serie D registration, a group of investors led by Marco Di Vincenzo formed S.S.D. Fidelis Andria 2018 as a reincarnation of S.S. Fidelis Andria 1928 to assume its sporting title.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The reborn club was admitted to Serie D.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2021, Fidelis Andria was admitted to play in [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") to fill a league vacancy. The club played two seasons in the Serie C before being relegated back to Serie D in 2023.

## Colors and crest

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidelis_Andria_2018&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Colors and crest")]

The team's colors are blue and white. The club crest, representing a rampant lion, is based on elements from the city of [Andria](/wiki/Andria "Andria")'s badge.
